name: Moorea
summary: Moorea is a heart-shaped tropical island in the South Pacific, a short ferry ride from Tahiti. Towering green volcanic peaks rise straight out of a warm turquoise lagoon full of friendly reef sharks, stingrays and sea turtles.
funFacts: From a plane Moorea looks like a giant green heart, made by an old volcano that collapsed into the sea about 1.5 million years ago.
Mount Mou'a Puta has a famous hole right through the top, and Polynesian legend says a god's spear punched it clean through the rock.
Each winter humpback whales swim thousands of kilometres from icy Antarctica to Moorea's warm lagoons to give birth to their calves.
In Stingray Bay you can stand in waist-deep water while smooth, friendly stingrays glide gently around your legs.
The name Moorea means 'yellow lizard' in Tahitian, from an old story about a magical egg that hatched a bright yellow lizard.
thingsToDo: Drive up to the Belvedere Lookout for an amazing view of Cook's Bay, Opunohu Bay and Mount Rotui in the middle.
Snorkel with friendly stingrays and small blacktip reef sharks in the shallow turquoise lagoon at Stingray Bay.
Hop on a whale-watching boat from July to November to spot mother humpback whales and their playful calves.
Splash and build sandcastles at Temae Beach, with white sand, palm trees and the island of Tahiti right across the sea.
Hike or ride up Magic Mountain for a panorama of the reef, the lagoon and the jagged peaks of the island.
Watch Polynesian dancers, drummers and fire-spinners put on a show at the Tiki Village cultural centre.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean traveller that visits Moorea every July to November to have its calves.

wildlife.name: Blacktip Reef Shark
wildlife.description: Small shy shark with black-tipped fins that zips around the shallow lagoon.

wildlife.name: Pink Whipray
wildlife.description: Big smooth stingray that glides over sandy lagoon floors looking for snacks.

wildlife.name: Green Sea Turtle
wildlife.description: Calm reptile with a beautiful patterned shell that grazes on seagrass in the lagoon.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Tahitian, the language of Moorea's Polynesian people.

images.alt: Aerial view of Moorea's two heart-shaped bays surrounded by green volcanic peaks and turquoise sea.
View from Belvedere Lookout showing Opunohu Bay and Cook's Bay with jungle peaks between them.
Mount Rotui rising between Opunohu Bay and Cook's Bay on Moorea, seen from the Belvedere.
The pointed peak of Mount Mou'a Puta, with its famous hole through the summit, seen from Cook's Bay.
Bright turquoise lagoon water lapping a sandy Moorea shore with palm trees and blue sky.
White sand and turquoise water at Temae Beach on Moorea, with the island of Tahiti across the sea.
A humpback whale and its calf swimming in the clear blue water near Moorea.
A small blacktip reef shark gliding through the shallow lagoon at Moorea.
A pink whipray stingray gliding over the sandy floor of the Moorea lagoon.
A green sea turtle gliding through the clear blue water around Moorea.
